summary: Keyword co-occurrence in minified bundle requires human review
details: A pattern rule fired on index.js citing co-occurrence of require('child_process'), an HTTP GET, and identifier tokens within the same minified file. No traced code path was confirmed that would tie these primitives into an exfiltration or remote-execution flow, and no attacker-controlled destination, credential read, or lifecycle hook was observed. The pattern alone — child_process import next to an HTTP call inside a single-line bundle — is a weak signal that legitimately appears in build/runtime tooling as well as in attack code. A human reviewer should de-minify index.js and confirm whether the HTTP destination is hardcoded attacker infrastructure, whether process arguments are constructed from remote response bodies, and whether any of this fires at install or require time.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
